question.text: Within how many days from the end of the financial year must the audit report of an InvIT be submitted to the stock exchanges?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under InvIT Regulations, the Investment Manager shall ensure that the audit of accounts of the InvIT by the auditor is done not less than once in a year and such report is submitted to the stock exchange(s) within sixty days of end of financial year ending March 31st. **The Trap:** 90 days (3 months) is the timeline for submitting the annual report to unit holders and stock exchanges, not the audit report. 45 days relates to half-yearly financial information. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Annual audit report to exchanges = 60 days; Annual report to unit holders = 3 months (90 days) from FY end.
